node_types.description: A raw on-chain account (EOA or contract) identified by a 20-byte address.
node_types.label: Address
node_types.name: address

node_types.description: A bridge contract moving value between Gnosis Chain and other networks.
node_types.label: Bridge
node_types.name: bridge

node_types.description: A Circles v2 avatar — a human, group, or organisation identity in the Circles trust graph.
node_types.label: Circles Avatar
node_types.name: circles_avatar

node_types.description: A Gnosis Pay wallet (relayer-owned Safe backing a Gnosis Pay card).
node_types.label: Gpay Wallet
node_types.name: gpay_wallet

node_types.description: A Dune/crawler project or protocol label attached to an address.
node_types.label: Project Label
node_types.name: project_label

node_types.description: Sector-by-day composite grain used by cross-sector daily rollups.
node_types.label: Sector Day
node_types.name: sector_day

node_types.description: Cross-sector pseudonymous user identifier (pseudonymize_address hash space).
node_types.label: User Pseudonym
node_types.name: user_pseudonym

node_types.description: A consensus-layer validator, keyed by validator index / withdrawal credentials.
node_types.label: Validator
node_types.name: validator

address_labeled_as.description: The int_crawlers_data_labels model consolidates and categorizes crawler data labels for various blockchain projects, enabling efficient analysis of project types and their associated attributes.

bridge_user_flows.description:
Address-grain bridge flows. Joins whitelisted transfers against the
dune labels set filtered on bridge projects to extract one row per
(date, bridge_contract, user_address, token, direction). Enables the
Graph Explorer to render (address -> bridge) edges that the aggregate
chain-level bridge models do not expose.

circles_avatar_balances.description:
Latest per-(avatar, token) CRC balance snapshot with an is_wrapped flag
indicating whether the token_address is an ERC-20 wrapper. Materialised
daily from fct_execution_circles_v2_avatar_balances_daily on the last
completed day, joined to the distinct wrapper registry from
int_execution_circles_v2_wrappers. The matching api_ view is a thin
passthrough for dashboard consumption.

circles_invitation.description:
Per-avatar identity for Circles v2: on-chain registration metadata
(avatar type, on-chain name, inviter, token_id, registration timestamp)
joined to the most recently fetched IPFS profile from
int_execution_circles_v2_avatar_metadata (display name,
preview/image URLs, description, current digest / CID / gateway URL,
fetched_at). Backs the "Avatar Identity" panel on the Circles dashboard.

circles_trust.description: API view of current active Circles v2 trust relations.

deposit_to_validator.description: The `int_GBCDeposit_deposists_daily` view aggregates daily deposit amounts and withdrawal credentials from GBC deposit events to support financial analysis and reporting.

gpay_ownership.description: Current owner snapshot for Gnosis Pay Safes. Thin filter over int_execution_safes_current_owners (the generic Safe ownership model) so post-setup owner mutations (AddedOwner / RemovedOwner / ChangedThreshold) are reflected, not just the creation-time SafeSetup snapshot. One row per (pay_wallet, owner) pair.

lending_user_to_reserve.description: Current Aave V3 lending positions per user with supply APY from the yields opportunities table. One row per (user, reserve) with positive balance.

pool_contains_token.description: Individual LP add/remove/collect events across all protocols on Gnosis Chain. One row per (event, token) — not pivoted to wide format because Balancer pools can hold 2–8 tokens, making a fixed-width schema incorrect. Each protocol's event decoding lives in its own staging model (stg_pools__dex_liquidity_*). This model unions them, enriches with token metadata, adds USD prices (ASOF join), and joins execution.transactions for tx_from/tx_to. The tx join uses an IN subquery so ClickHouse builds a small hash set of event tx hashes and probes the large transactions table, rather than loading the full right side into memory. Protocols: Uniswap V3, Swapr V3, Balancer V2, Balancer V3. event_type values: 'mint' (add), 'burn' (remove), or 'collect' (claim fees; V3 only). amount_raw is always positive; event_type conveys direction. Includes V3 tick range (tick_lower/tick_upper) and signed liquidity delta for position-level tracking and in-range analysis. When called with start_month/end_month vars each protocol model filters to that month window; otherwise falls back to the incremental filter.
question_synonyms: which tokens does a pool contain

safe_ownership.description:
Current owner set per Safe.

For each (safe_address, owner) pair, take the latest event by
(block_number, log_index). If the latest event is safe_setup or
added_owner, the owner is current; if removed_owner, the row is
dropped. Threshold is the latest safe_setup / changed_threshold per
Safe, denormalized onto every owner row.

`became_owner_at` is the timestamp of the most recent event that
added (or set up) this owner — for an owner who was removed and then
re-added, it is the re-add time, not the original setup time.

Cheap to fully rebuild every run; no incremental strategy needed.

token_transfers.description: The `int_execution_transfers_whitelisted_daily` model aggregates daily whitelisted token transfer data, including deposits, withdrawals, and transfers involving specific tokens, to support analysis of token movement activity within a defined time range.

validator_controlled_by.description:
Thin projection over int_consensus_validators_labels that derives the controlling
EVM address from each validator's withdrawal_credentials (concatenation of '0x'
with the trailing 20 bytes via substring(withdrawal_credentials, 27, 40)). Saves
downstream queries the substring extraction. Only rows with a 0x01-type credential
expose a withdrawal_address — older 0x00-type BLS credentials return NULL.
